Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'Yellow bar + red squares - likely timer/progress'}

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The block is now at rows 25-29, cols 14-18. The orange part is at [25,14]-[26,18] and the blue part is at [27,14]-[29,18]. The white cross is at rows ~31-33, cols ~19-22.
The block is now to the LEFT of the white cross. The white cross is to the right of the block. The block is at cols 14-18, and the white cross is at cols 19-22.
Looking at the image, the block seems to be at the leftmost position of the corridor. The white cross is just to the right of the block.
I think the goal might be to have the block positioned so that it's adjacent to or overlapping the white cross. Currently the block is at rows 25-29, cols 14-18, and the white cross is at rows ~31-33, cols ~19-22.
Let me try RIGHT to move the block back toward the white cross, or try DOWN to move the block down to align vertically with the white cross.
Actually, looking more carefully at the image, the block is now at the far left of the corridor, and the white cross is to the right of it. The block can't move further LEFT (it's at the wall). Let me try RIGHT to move it back toward the white cross, or DOWN to align vertically.
Wait, looking at the image again, the block is at the left edge of the gray room. The white cross is to the right of the block. The corridor continues to the right. Let me try RIGHT to move the block back toward the white cross.
Actually, I think the goal might be to position the block so that it OVERLAPS the white cross. The white cross is at rows ~31-33, cols ~19-22. The block is at rows 25-29, cols 14-18. The block needs to move DOWN to rows 30-34 and RIGHT to cols 19-23 to overlap the white cross.
But wait, LEFT was blocked from rows 30-34 earlier. So if I move DOWN, I might not be able to move RIGHT.
Let me try RIGHT first to see if the block can move back toward the white cross from its current position.
